title: Apparatuses, systems, and methods for inline injection of gases into liquids
abstract: A gas injection system for injecting a gas into a liquid to form a solution includes a flow channel that conveys a liquid from an upstream inlet configured to receive the liquid and a downstream outlet configured to dispense the solution, sparger positioned in the flow channel, a solution pressure detection device configured to sense a pressure of the solution in the flow channel, and a liquid valve configured to regulate flow of the liquid in the flow channel based on the pressure sensed by the solution pressure detection device. The sparger is configured to inject the gas into the liquid through the porous surface as the liquid flows across the surface.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A gas injection device for injecting a gas into a liquid to form a solution, the gas injection device comprising: a flow channel that conveys a liquid from an upstream inlet configured to receive the liquid to a downstream outlet configured to dispense the solution; and a sparger having a porous surface positioned in the flow channel such that the liquid flows across the porous surface, the sparger configured to inject the gas into the liquid through the porous surface as the liquid flows across the porous surface. 2. The gas injection device of claim 1, wherein the porous surface is elongated such that the liquid flows tangentially across the porous surface and the gas injects transversely into the liquid. 3. The gas injection device of claim 2, wherein the flow channel and the sparger are configured such that the liquid flows under laminar flow conditions across the porous surface. 4. The gas injection device of claim 3, wherein the sparger comprises a non-porous surface positioned in the flow channel upstream of the porous surface, wherein the flow channel and the sparger are configured such that the liquid flows under non-laminar flow conditions across the non-porous surface. 5. The gas injection device of claim 4, wherein the flow channel and the sparger are configured such that the liquid flows under non-laminar flow conditions downstream of the porous surface, which causes the gas to remain in the solution. 6. The gas injection device of claim 2, wherein the flow channel comprises an inner perimeteral surface that extends along a center axis; and wherein the porous surface is tapered and is concentric with the inner perimeteral surface. 7. The gas injection device of claim 6, wherein a distance between the porous surface and the inner perimeteral surface are spaced apart a distance of 1/16 inches. 8. The gas injection device of claim 1, wherein the porous surface comprises an expanded mesh. 9. The gas injection device of the claim 8, wherein the expanded mesh comprises a plurality of pores having a diameter between 0.2 microns and 2.0 microns. 10. A gas injection system for injecting a gas into a liquid to form a solution, the gas injection system comprising: a flow channel that conveys a liquid from an upstream inlet configured to receive the liquid and a downstream outlet configured to dispense the solution; a sparger positioned in the flow channel such that the liquid flows across the porous surface, the sparger configured to inject the gas into the liquid through the porous surface as the liquid flows across the surface; a solution pressure detection device configured to sense a pressure of the solution in the flow channel; and a liquid valve configured to regulate flow of the liquid in the flow channel based on the pressure sensed by the solution pressure detection device. 11. The gas injection system according to claim 10, wherein the liquid valve increases the flow of the liquid when the solution pressure detection device senses a decrease in the pressure of the solution. 12. The gas injection system according to claim 10, further comprising: a gas valve configured to regulate the flow of the gas in the sparger based on the pressure sensed by the solution pressure detection device. 13. The gas injection system according to claim 12, wherein the gas valve is configured to increase the flow of the gas to thereby increase the pressure of the gas above the pressure of the liquid such that the gas injects into the liquid through the porous surface of the sparger. 14. The gas injection system according to claim 13, wherein the gas valve is configured to maintain the pressure of the gas substantially equal to the pressure of the liquid to thereby prevent the liquid from entering the sparger through the porous surface. 15. The gas injection system according to claim 14, wherein the liquid valve and gas valve are configured to maintain a dynamic pressure delta range between the pressure of the gas and the pressure of the liquid of 0.5 to 5.0 pounds per square inch. 16. The gas injection system according to claim 15, further comprising a controller that controls the liquid valve and the gas valve based on signals received from the solution pressure detection device. 17. The gas injection system according to claim 16, wherein the gas injection system is a closed-loop system wherein the controller continuously receives signals from the solution pressure detection device and continuously controls the liquid valve and the gas valve to maintain a selected pressure of the solution 18. The gas injection system according to claim 10, further comprising a backpressure device configured to apply a backpressure on the solution to thereby maintain consistency of the solution downstream of the sparger. 19. The gas injection system according to claim 18, wherein the backpressure device comprises a porous restrictor plate. 20. A method of injecting a gas into a liquid comprising: selecting a flow channel that conveys a liquid from an upstream inlet configured to receive the liquid to a downstream outlet configured to dispense the solution; positioning a sparger having a porous surface in the flow channel such that the liquid flows across the porous surface and injects the gas into the liquid through the porous surface; sensing the pressure of the solution in the flow channel with a solution pressure detection device; and 

 regulating the flow of the liquid with a liquid valve based on the pressure sensed by the solution pressure detection device. 21. The method according to claim 20, further comprising: regulating the flow of the gas with a gas valve based on the solution pressure sensed by the solution pressure detection device. 22. The method according to claim 21, further comprising: positioning a backpressure device in the flow channel downstream of the sparger such that the backpressure device applies a backpressure on the solution. 23. The method according to claim 22, further comprising: controlling the liquid valve and the gas valve with a controller configured to receive signals from the solution pressure detection device and the gas pressure detection device such that the pressure of the gas is greater than the pressure of the liquid whereby the gas injects into the liquid.
